# llms.txt

Project: Legado Digital / Colegio Tancredo Neves
Repository root: c:\xampp\htdocs\cecmtancredoneves
Type: Static educational website
Language: pt-BR content, file naming mostly lowercase and path-based

## Purpose

This project is a digital exhibition website for student work from Colegio Tancredo Neves.
It showcases photos, subject pages, slides, books, handouts, and interactive educational content.
There is no backend and no build pipeline. The site is composed of static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, local assets, and PDF viewers.

## Runtime model

- Served as a static site from XAMPP or any static host.
- Main local root is `c:\xampp\htdocs\cecmtancredoneves`.
- No package manager, bundler, framework, or server-side application code.
- Most page data is hardcoded directly inside HTML files through inline JavaScript arrays.

## Main routes

- `/index.html`: home page with hero, image slider, and featured subject entry
- `/materias.html`: subject index page
- `/mural.html`: photo wall
- `/slide.html`: presentation listing page
- `/acervo.html`: unified archive for photos, slides, books, and handouts
- `/sobre.html`: project/about page
- `/visualizador-slide.html`: slide PDF viewer
- `/visualizador-pdf.html`: generic PDF viewer
- `/visualizador-livro.html`: book-style PDF viewer with page flip behavior

## Subject routes

- `/materias/educacao-financeira/index.html`
- `/materias/educacao-financeira/historia-do-dinheiro-brasil.html`
- `/materias/robotiva/index.html`
- `/materias/filosofia/index.html`
- `/materias/maker/index.html`

## Asset folders

- `/image/`: photo gallery images used by home slider, mural, and archive
- `/slide/`: presentation PDFs
- `/livros/`: PDF books
- `/apostilas/`: PDF handouts
- `/audio/`: sound effects used by the book viewer
- `/assets/image/`: icons and favicon assets

## CSS architecture

Entry point:
- `/css/style.css`

Imported shared layers:
- `/css/layout.css`: navbar, footer, container, shared grid structure
- `/css/components.css`: buttons, cards, media blocks, shared UI components
- `/css/pages.css`: page-specific layouts and hero sections

Additional page-specific styles:
- `/css/acervo.css`
- `/css/slide.css`
- `/css/visualizador.css`
- `/css/visualizador-livro.css`

## JavaScript architecture

- `/js/main.js`: shared mobile menu toggle
- `/js/loader.js`: helper loader for the book viewer
- `/js/turn.min.js`: page flip dependency used by `visualizador-livro.html`

Viewer dependencies loaded from CDN inside HTML:
- PDF.js for slide and PDF rendering
- jQuery plus Turn.js flow for the book-like viewer
- Font Awesome icons in viewer pages

## Important content conventions

- Presentation assets must use the folder name `slide/`.
- The presentation page is `slide.html`.
- The presentation viewer is `visualizador-slide.html`.
- Do not reintroduce the old `slaides` naming pattern.
- Navigation links are duplicated across many HTML files, so link changes usually need to be applied in multiple pages.
- Subject cards and archive entries are not generated from a CMS; they are maintained manually.

## Where content lists live

- `/index.html`: image slider file list
- `/slide.html`: presentation list and thumbnail generation
- `/acervo.html`: master archive lists for images, slides, books, and handouts

## Editing guidance for agents

- Prefer editing shared CSS files before adding inline styles.
- Keep existing visual identity: blue `#173557`, green `#68813c`, white backgrounds, rounded cards.
- Preserve static hosting compatibility. Avoid introducing Node, build tools, or framework-specific assumptions unless explicitly requested.
- When renaming folders or files, update all hardcoded paths in HTML and inline JavaScript.
- If adding new assets, verify whether they also need to be added to `slide.html`, `acervo.html`, or home page sliders.
- If changing a shared route such as `slide.html` or a viewer page, review navbar links across root pages and subject pages.
- Book and handout flows use `visualizador-livro.html`.
- Presentation flows use `visualizador-slide.html`.
- Generic PDF navigation flows use `visualizador-pdf.html`.

## Maintenance notes

- `project_structure.md` is a generated structure snapshot and may need regeneration after large file moves.
- The repository may contain local content updates not reflected in README text.
- The project is content-heavy; asset path correctness matters more than code abstraction.
